# EventAlertMod Formal Clothing Rewrite: AI Context

## Project Summary

EventAlertMod (EAM) is a World of Warcraft AddOn for lightweight Halo,
Cooldown time and item cooldown. Cooling's Identity Is Simple: Display Matters
Attributes and item status are key icons without becoming WeakAuras clones.

This rewrite is only for the Retail server version of World of Warcraft, the Retail server version 12.x /
zh-TW_to_en.639bfac4a14f3a29340f8410cfac63e5:
Midnight Era's API is an expected derivative commodity. Existing Classic, Mystery of Panda Classic servers,
Cataclysm Classic, Wrath of the Lich King Classic, TBC, Era and select area Classic branches are
Only historical behavior, no new structures may be processed.

## Rewrite direction

The current source code tree retains much of the behavior but mixes compatibility
Branching, UI construction, halo scanning, cooling project, cache generation,
zh-TW_to_en.821ff188babe7b3c56c4d14374de4e10:
Special resources, slash directives, global variables, localization and frame layout
Same runtime surface. Rewrites should preserve useful data and make it available to users
images, while replacing internal structures with explicit modules.

Required target mods:

- `Core/Env.lua`
- `Core/Util.lua`
- `Core/Constants.lua`
- `Core/EventRouter.lua`
- `Core/Scheduler.lua`
- `Core/SavedVariables.lua`
- `Core/Performance.lua`
- `Services/AuraService.lua`
- `Services/CooldownService.lua`
- `Services/ItemCooldownService.lua`
- `Services/SpellInfoService.lua`
- `UI/IconPool.lua`
- `UI/Renderer.lua`
- `UI/Options.lua`
- `UI/Slash.lua`
- `Debug/DebugState.lua`
zh-TW_to_en.77234c72af1892822735e3bd4d4a5f1a:
- `Debug/PromptExport.lua`

## Non-negotiable boundaries

- There is no secret value to bypass.
- Unprotected data bypassed.
- No combat automation.
- No external dependencies.
- No complex user script engine.
- Intensive continuous scanning.
- There is no per-graph timer, per-scope timer, or per-project timer hardware.
## Simple principle
zh-TW_to_en.fcbbf84bd85c8ae19a943cd180824cb7:
EAM should be kept simple for general users: add text ID, enable alerts, see
icon, adjust group display options, and export compact debugging status
Available only.

## Existing discussion anchors

The previous ChatGPT discussion context is at:

- `DevDocument/ChatGPT/EventAlertMod_ChatGPT_Discussion_Context.md`

Important historical points from this document:

- EAM is not based on Ace3.
-Currently the directory uses `RequiredDeps: !Lib_ZYF`, but the rewrite target is not new
  External dependencies.
zh-TW_to_en.bbb0dc6f8a24eb1ae3404d926894b16a:
- `/eam opt` is the recorded setting command.
- Existing behaviors include self-Aura, target Aura, character cooldown, and items
  Cooldowns, tooltip symbols/item IDs, localization and optional debugging assistants.
- Older work has exceeded "Main/EventAlert_EAFun.lua" as a compatibility
Facade. Prospective agents must not imagine the document as the final architecture.

## First time passed review result
zh-TW_to_en.ebd9103f7946ea9322f1204087c21f45:
`Docs/01_ARCHITECTURE.md` contains the current file map and module
`Docs/03_STATE_SCHEMA.md` contains SavedVariables and global variables
`Docs/05_PERFORMANCE_GUIDE.md` contains candidate hot paths,
OnUpdate/C_Timer usage and distribution risks. `文件/07_MIGRATION_NOTES.md`
Contains behavioral migration notes.
zh-TW_to_en.10a10862ecbf17a7d5b8ba6ec33d97ba:

# Architecture

Current ## file mapping

Root:

- `EventAlertMod_Mainline.toc`: Formal clothing/main line directory, current interface
  `120000`, version `TWW_11.2.5_20251111`.
- `EventAlertMod_Cata.toc`, `EventAlertMod_Mists.toc`,
  `EventAlertMod_TBC.toc`, `EventAlertMod_Wrath.toc`: Unsupported legacy directories.
- `embeds.xml`: Load bundle library.
- `README.md`, `changelog.txt`: historical behavior and command context.
- `libs/`: Bundle `LibCustomGlow-1.0`, `LibDebug`, `LibStub`.
- `locale/`: `Localization.comm.lua`, `.en.lua`, `.tw.lua`, `.cn.lua`,
  `.kr.lua`, `.ru.lua`.
zh-TW_to_en.78779ecfa9e5ddf121a01e1035c42db2:
- `Images/`, `Sounds/`: Media assets.
- `DevDocument/`: Reference to URL and the previous ChatGPT context.
- `Classic/`, `TBC/`, `Wrath/`: Inherited sources are not supported.
- `Main/`: Current mainline implementation.

Current main line load equipment:
- `Main/EventAlertMod.xml`: Load the mainline Lua module and create it
  `EA_Main_Frame` / `EA_Version_Frame`.
- XML options panel:
  - `Main/EventAlert_Options.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_IconOptions.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_ClassAlerts.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_OtherAlerts.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_TargetAlerts.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_SCDAlerts.xml`
  - `Main/EventAlert_GroupAlerts.xml`
zh-TW_to_en.a750374344647dfd23005b87b4afcef0:
Current mainline Lua modules:

- `EventAlert_LoadDefault.lua`: Default `EA_Config2` value.
- `EventAlert_InitVar.lua`: initialize `EA_Config`, `EA_Position`, namespace
  Runtime tables, debug flags, class/specification global variables.
- `EventAlert_InitVar_DK.lua`: Death Knight runes/textures.
- `EventAlert_SpellItem.lua`: Large weapon/item data sheet.
- `EventAlert_CreateFrames.lua`: Create icons, auxiliary points, scrolling lists,
  Minimap button, group frame and special resource frame.
zh-TW_to_en.ff27266184691ea1d223025834250dc3:
- `EventAlert_SpellArray.lua`: category/default coordinate system and data loading.
- `EventAlert_Animation.lua`: Visual animation/background helper.
- `EventAlert_Util.lua`: printing assistant, tooltip assistant, halo search,
  Buff list flaws, glowing assistant, spell/talent checks.
- `EventAlert_EAFun.lua`: compatibility skin for scrolling/debugging, migration,
  Tooltips, conditions, timer text, grouped results, layouts and prompt bindings.
zh-TW_to_en.3d9f88497e8510e289b91e2309a1873c:
- `EventAlert_SlashCommand.lua`: `/eam` instruction processing.
- `EventAlert_GroupEvent.lua`: User-defined conditional/group alert checking.
- `EventAlert_Core.lua`: plugin loading, event registration/scheduling, most
  Top-level event handlers, lookups, version checks, update loops.
- `EventAlert_ItemSpellCache.lua`: Project-to-prop cache for browsers and large projects
  Scan.
- `EventAlert_Aura_Core.lua`: Full/incremental Aura cache helper.
zh-TW_to_en.bef3060dc3777f42e0b3230c43d8ef31:
- `EventAlert_Aura_Self.lua`: Player/pet Aura alerts and self-icon layout.
- `EventAlert_Aura_Target.lua`: Target Aura alert and target icon layout.
- `EventAlert_Cooldown.lua`: Asset/item cooldown and SCD layout.
- `EventAlert_SpecialPower.lua`: runes, combination points, category resources,
  Frame of life bloom, vitality and other special powers.
- `EventAlert_API.lua`: API alias layer, currently not in the XML loading list.
- `EventAlert_Options.lua`: Options UI logic and group event editor.
zh-TW_to_en.6bc3a119e0a4872aacafd1a0653810eb:
- `EventAlert_IconOptions.lua`: Icon option UI, button point movement, font/position
settings.
- `EventAlert_ImportExport.lua`: Import/export prototype, currently annotated
  Output in XML format.

Current ## XML/UI template usage

The static XML panel uses these template families:

- `UIPanelButtonTemplate`
- `UICheckButtonTemplate`
- `OptionsSliderTemplate`
- `UIDropDownMenuTemplate`
- `UIPanelScrollFrameTemplate`
- `InputBoxTemplate`
- `BackdropTemplate`
- `GameFontNormal`
- `GameFontHighlight`
- `EA_SpellEditTextTemplate`

Dynamic Lua framework is built and used:
zh-TW_to_en.e7765cdb29ba0db37b92d7fecd8d4cab:
- `CreateFrame("Frame", ...)` for alert icons, items, inventory containers, groups
Frame and minimap buttons.
- `CreateFrame("Cooldown", ..., "CooldownFrameTemplate")` for cooling slide
  One frame.
- `CreateFrame("ScrollFrame", ..., "UIPanelScrollFrameTemplate")` for spells
  List and import/export panels.
- `CreateFrame("EditBox", ..., "InputBoxTemplate")` and XML edit boxes
  Templates for code IDs and import/export text.
- `CreateFrame("Button", ..., "UIPanelButtonTemplate")` and XML options
  button.
zh-TW_to_en.4670fa4d9190e3fe1dce07c46648337d:
- `CreateFontString(..., "GameFontNormal")` and related game fonts.
- Traversal of `Lib_ZYF:SetBackdrop` and direct `BackdropTemplate` call background
Use in initialization import/export code.

Rewrite rules: UI templates belong to `UI/IconPool`, `UI/Renderer` and
`UI/Options`. Services cannot directly create frames or change frame layouts.

Current ## Responsibilities

Current course number responsibilities are mixed:

- `Core.lua` has loading sequence, event registration, event routing, and functions
zh-TW_to_en.35fc7cd2b16e6acb5f8e7ec50c729372:
Handles programs, looks for scans, version checks, and rolls back update schedules.
- Aura services are divided into "Aura_Core", "Aura_Self" and "Aura_Target",
  But it's still direct rendering and scheduling.
- Cooling service performs cooling query, cooling item query,
  Rendering delays, and delayed updates in a mod.
- Frame creation and renderer behavior are distributed in "CreateFrames",
  `EAFun`, `Aura_*`, `Cooldown`, `SpecialPower` and option modules.
zh-TW_to_en.0f3501b8bc4f69c0db66417b3f3c49eb:
- SavedVariables are initialized directly in the global table, consumption mode
  Versioning or validation boundaries.
- Localization uses global strings and mixes into UI settings.

## Target module mapping

Core:

- `Env`: plug-in mode namespace, local API alias, build/flavor protection, production server only
  guard.
- `Util`: contribution assistant, pool, backing table API, enumeration assistant,
  assert.
zh-TW_to_en.11cd0ba6c00cb30a193cc298a34a1ae7:
- `Default`: Lists enumerations, event names, status codes, schema versions.
- `EventRouter`: One frame, one `OnEvent`, event to the module's schedule.
-`Scheduler`: one `OnUpdate`, inconsistent queuing, low-frequency fallback, none
  Timer for each icon.
- `SavedVariables`: Defaults, migrations, validations, versioning modes.
- `Performance`: FPS/Combat throttling, optional analysis, shared pool.

Serve:
zh-TW_to_en.8aeb030eacd4fa38a89b8b69669bff50:
- `AuraService`: Safe Player/Target Halo Bracket and Cache Owner.
- `CooldownService`: Automatic cooling and normalized cooling status.
- `ItemCooldownService`: Item cooldown queue and optional incremental cache.
- `SpellInfoService`: spell name/icon/link cache.
- `ClassPowerService`: Player level special powers (Holy Power, Shards, Combo Points, Chi, Arcane Charges) and the corresponding number in the center.
zh-TW_to_en.2f023f750084088abbaaba04190b67a8:
- `GroundEffectService`: Ground effects induction (SPELL_CAST_SUCCESS) with dual duration (dynamic tooltip grab vs. manual input) timer tracking.
- `TotemService`: The Shaman Totem slot is monitored using C_Totems.GetTotemInfo.

User profile:

- `IconPool`: Retrieve buttons, textures, cooldown areas and FontStrings.
- `Renderer`: only consumes standardized rendering state; no data is obtained.
- `Options`: Simple configuration panel.
zh-TW_to_en.964aa9eca95b6d1e0f5b40d584b6f0d5:
- `Slash`: `/eam` command parser and detect mis-exported commands.

False detection:
- `DebugState`: Concise execution phase snapshot splitting facts, derived states,
Human notes, border vigilance, and the environment.
- `PromptExport`: A continuous sequence of output similar to JSON.

## Data flow

1. The Blizzard event enters `EventRouter`.
2. The router schedules to a certain or set service.
3. The service reads the secure official service API and updates its execution time status.
zh-TW_to_en.c08f93e4039283cd3dcf96104a6446e3:
4. Service release or mark "AlertState" record.
5. `Renderer` receives the alarm status and updates the pool icon.
6. The "Scheduler" only handles refresh, rollback sampling and debugging bursts.
7. __​​​​EAMCODE_0__ is read/migrated on load and only configuration changes are read.

## Activity process

The main event should only include necessary formal attire events such as:

- `Player Login`
- `Player enters the world`
- `PLAYER_REGEN_ENABLED`
- `PLAYER_REGEN_DISABLED`
- `PLAYER_TARGET_CHANGED`
- `UNIT_AURA`
zh-TW_to_en.30e895f817a5f9e3d437d6c33d15c92f:
- `SPELL_UPDATE_COOLDOWN`
- `SPELL_UPDATE_CHARGES`
- `BAG_UPDATE_COOLDOWN`
- `UNIT_SPELLCAST_SUCCEEDED`
- Power/resource events only occur when special resource UI is enabled

There is no "RegisterAllEvents".

## UI rendering process

The renderer input is "IconRenderState", not the raw Aura/cooldown API data. Renderer:

- Get the icon from "IconPool";
- Change the texture's name, time, timer, cooldown, glow and alpha simply after writing;
zh-TW_to_en.d1bd364d8b850cbed3dbed4f949bb0a8:
- Change the layout in batches and hide the parent, then show it once;
- Avoid changing `ClearAllPoints` and `SetPoint` without layout keys;
- Never query C_* API.

## Scheduling planning process

A scheduler framework tracks jobs in digital time. Work is a reusable record,
Configuration points are not blocked. Fallback polling must be low frequency and
In combat/low FPS or fail throttling unless it protects correctness.

## Retail server change plan only
zh-TW_to_en.a4e5c07b16a24c886e33feeabfd1cf3b:
1. Import new module components under `Core/`, `Services/` and load the sequence.
   "UI/" and "DEBUG/".
2. When creating a new table, only keep the old "Main/" table as a migration/reference
   architecture.
3. Migrate SavedVariables to the versioned profile and alert list model.
4. Port localized strings into independent localization modules.
5. Implement `EventRouter` and `Scheduler` before porting services.
zh-TW_to_en.cede239747a68056587ff5bcd18cce0c:
6. Transplant player Aura, target Aura, character cooldown, and cooldown service items.
7. Implement the pooled renderer to place the frame and create the removal service module.
8. Rebuild slash directives and debug exporter for new services.
9. Remove legacy TOC and Classic/MOP compatibility branches from active loads.
10. Perform static inspection followed immediately by formal garment verification.
zh-TW_to_en.25b7dfa0df1896219a833c66f4f151ab:

# Retail server API boundary

## Retail server 12.x assumptions

This rewrite targets Retail 12.x/Midnight-era API. All API notes in this file are considered file assumptions unless they have been loaded and tested on WoW Retail.

Prefer namespaces:

- `C_AddOns`
- `C_Spell`
- `C_Item`
- `C_UnitAuras`
- `C_TooltipInfo`
- `AuraUtil`
- `C_Timer` Only via central dispatcher or explicit non-thermal settings
zh-TW_to_en.3c75716adbdcbe82823248d6f7f8cf93:
The legacy decompression return phase content layer is not retained unless there are clear, strict, and documented Retail server security fallbacks.

## Taint Control Policy
The Warcraft Wiki's safe execution/taint document states that AddOn and `/script` are untrusted sources; once taint enters the protected/secure path, Blizzard UI actions may be starved during combat. EAM Avoiding polluting modifications must be considered an architectural boundary, not just a bug.

Implementation rules:
zh-TW_to_en.0748711e863eceed12e1eb2465246a1e:
- No hooking, overwriting, redefining or monkey-patching Blizzard secure/protected functions, FrameXML core functions, action buttons, unit frames, nameplates, spell casting, targeting, item usage related paths.
- Do not modify the protected frame's properties, parent, anchors, size, visibility, templates or click behavior in combat.
- Not identifying secret/protected values, runtime caches, debugging objects or AddOn callbacks that could pollute the Blizzard framework's security chain.
zh-TW_to_en.4b97bce6d84d3d459dd3e24a88df155e:
- EventRouter uses orphan frames; the frames are rendered for display only and do not assume safe operation or protected interaction.
- When UIPalent frames are required, limit them to unprotected display purposes; if `InCombatLockdown()` is true, defer structural UI changes.
- No use of `forceinsecure`, no attempts to bypass the taint, and no workarounds to suppress Blizzard blocking actions.
- When detecting stains, blocked actions, or combat lock errors, log them to `Docs/15_DEVELOPMENT_ISSUE_LOG.md`.

Rewrite rules:
zh-TW_to_en.b8e4d007a6e174e50e60420d1b826c9e:
- Aura facts must come from securely structured Retail server Aura data, anchored by stable `NeverSecret` `AuraInstanceID`.
- **Block Halo Integration**: For unwanted or spam Halos, please utilize native "C_UnitAuras.AddBlockedAura(unit, auraInstanceID)" to delegate filtering to the C++ engine. This method (`AllowedWhenUntainted`) must only be called if the plugin's execution path is completely untainted.
zh-TW_to_en.d9a89b040158702e8df38ac854351f50:
- **Native Dual Pipe Duration**: Preference for retrieving the C++ black box `DurationObject` via `C_UnitAuras.GetAuraDuration` and serving it directly to native gadgets (`CooldownFrame:SetCooldownFromDurationObject` and __EA__MCODE_13).
- **Pandemic Prediction**: DoT refresh checks must compare `C_UnitAuras.GetRefreshExtendedDuration(unit, auraInstanceID)` with `GetAuraBaseDuration * 1.3` in combat to bypass the restricted number `timeLeft` check.
zh-TW_to_en.2726dfef0c9163f82ff1f0c6210eeb58:
- If a value is secret/protected/display-only, mark `boundaryLimited` and do not force it to pass normal Lua comparison or table key logic.
- Don't keep the extensive legacy UnitAura unpacking mapping as architecture.

## Current specialization and localization API review

Current main line reference:

- `GetSpecializationInfoForClassID`
- `GetClassInfo`
- `GetSpecializationInfo`

Rewrite rules:

- **DYNAMIC LOCALIZATION**: Use native API to query for match strings that are dynamically aligned with the client's current language setting, rather than hardcoding localization specialization or class names in the configuration UI.
zh-TW_to_en.26757ddb3e2aeeec15cdd44066c76210: - **Canonical dropdown filtering**: Map category tags to category IDs (use static enum mapping, e.g. `CLASS_TOKEN_TO_ID` matches WoW category ID). Dynamically retrieve the canonical name via `GetSpecializationInfoForClassID(classID, specIndex)` (where `classID` is a number between 1 and 13 and `specIndex` is a number between 1 and 4).
zh-TW_to_en.4ba8c114df6c7fe80ba8154a1f7266fe:
- **Dual Path Fallback**: When native localization API returns `nil` or null, use a static localization table (`EAM.L`) to implement reliable fallback mapping to ensure that UI elements always have readable names.

## Secret/Protected Value Policy

When data is not secure or available:

- **Four security checks API**:
  - `issecretvalue(value)`: Check if a value is classified as secret.
zh-TW_to_en.406cd71012f76360565e0005726ef713:
- `canaccessvalue(value)`: Determine whether the current context has permission to read a certain value.
  - `canaccesstable(table)`: Evaluates whether the table's keys and values ​​are readable.
  - `issecrettable(table)` / `hasanysecretvalues(table)`: Check whether the table structure is restricted or contains secrets.
- **Table Index Protection (Critical)**:
- AddOn MUST NOT index standard Lua tables using unvalidated keys that may be "secret values" (e.g. returning `spellId` or `text` during combat restrictions).
zh-TW_to_en.7df3632d79c10c698eb3a7f0ea3a450e:
- Attempting to index a table using a key produces a fatal error: "Attempting to index a table that cannot be indexed using a key."
  - Always use "if not issecretvalue(key) and canaccesstable(tbl) then ... end" to protect table lookups.
- **Data-Driven Tooltips and Privacy Defense**:
  - A direct `C_TooltipInfo` query (`GetUnitBuffByAuraInstanceID`) in the combat return structure `TooltipData` may be marked as a "secret table".
zh-TW_to_en.0d6254e8e55347ae574283405b7cf540:
- When parsing static values ​​from `line.leftText`, be sure to use "if text and not issecretvalue(text) and canaccessvalue(text) then ... end" to prevent secret propagation.
- **None `TooltipUtil.SurfaceArgs`**: In 12.x/Midnight, the tooltip table is displayed natively. The `TooltipUtil.SurfaceArgs` helper has been completely removed; trying to call it throws a fatal "nil value" Lua error.
- Continue rendering safe state such as icon/name if available.
- Use timer mode `protected`, `displayOnly` or `unknown`.
zh-TW_to_en.e699d4d1ef4c9025569de5ad9f81d936:
- Added boundary warning for debugging status.
- Schedule non-combat refreshes only when it is safe to do so.
- Never confuse guesswork with fact.
- Never pass unsafe values ​​into the secure/protected UI chain.

## Intentionally avoided API/mode

- Classic API branch.
- MOP/Cata/Wrath/TBC API returns the mapping.
- `RegisterAllEvents`.
- Tooltip scan as normal source (only use it for low frequency static duration crawl fallback).
zh-TW_to_en.90fdc9918a58ec277041350a41d525f9:
- `TooltipUtil.SurfaceArgs` usage (always let the native engine display parameters).
- Scan a large number of items ID/combat during login.
- Each icon `SetScript("OnUpdate")`.
- Duplicate `C_Timer.After(function() ...)` chain in hot path.
- Configured external frame dependencies.
- `forceinsecure` or any taint workaround to bypass or suppress blocked operations.

#Module contract

## Ownership Rules

- `SavedVariables` holds persistent configuration tables and migrations.
- Services own runtime facts.
- `Renderer` holds the rendered UI state.
- `IconPool` owns the frame object.
- `Scheduler` has the job he deserves.
- `DebugState` owns the debug/session snapshot.
- Static constants may be frozen. Runtime state and SavedVariables must not
  is frozen.
zh-TW_to_en.8ebd1b0f516112cefc22e0cc68e12f11:
- Each auto-loaded source file must start with a module comment block
  Document purpose, design philosophy, ownership, mutation boundaries, and
  Maintenance precautions. Please keep this review up to date as mod contracts change.
  See "Docs/12_CODE_COMMENTARY_GUIDE.md".

- Plug-in name and namespace

Output:

- Plug-in namespace
- Local API alias table

Mutation:
- Can initialize the plug-in namespace once
- MUST NOT CHANGE SavedVariables

Input:

- Original Lua/WoW table API

Output:

- `CreateTable`, `FreezeTable`, `IsFrozen`, Pool Assistant, Secure Erase /release,
  Stable enumeration helper, debugging assertions
- Secret/protected value safe reading assistant:
  - `readSafeField`
  - `readSafeScalar`
  - `markBoundary`
  - `appendBoundaryWarning`
  - `clearTimer`

Mutation:

- Have only secondary local pools

## Core/Constants

Input:

Output:

- Freeze enumeration table, module name, event name, status constant, mode
  Version, sentinel constants such as `UNKNOWN` and `EMPTY`

Output:

#Performance Guide

## Hot path candidates

Current mainline hot path candidates:

- `Main/EventAlert_Core.lua`
  - Event dispatch and handlers
  - `COMBAT_LOG_EVENT_UNFILTERED`
  - `UNIT_AURA`
  - `BAG_UPDATE_COOLDOWN`
  - `SPELL_UPDATE_COOLDOWN`
  - `SPELL_UPDATE_CHARGES`
  - `SPELL_UPDATE_USABLE`
  - `ACTIONBAR_UPDATE_COOLDOWN`
  - Use code /coroutine to perform a lookup scan
- `Main/EventAlert_Aura_Self.lua`
  - `Buffs_Update`
  - `OnUpdate`
  - `PositionFrames`
- `Main/EventAlert_Aura_Target.lua`
  - `TarBuffs_Update`
  - `OnTarUpdate`
  - `TarPositionFrames`
- `Main/EventAlert_Cooldown.lua`
  - `OnSCDUpdate`
  - `ScdBuffs_Update`
zh-TW_to_en.04f596ce8bdd4388e2909933924baad2:
- `UpdateScdFrame`
  - `ScdPositionFrames`
- `Main/EventAlert_ItemSpellCache.lua`
  - Item range scan builder
- `Main/EventAlert_SpecialPower.lua`
- Resource /power updated
  - rune OnUpdate script
- `Main/EventAlert_CreateFrames.lua`
  - Frame creation and scrolling list generation
- `Main/EventAlert_EAFun.lua`
  - Layout configuration, tool tips, timer text, debugging label assistant

## Current OnUpdate / C_Timer usage

Observed mainline usage:

- `EventAlert_Core.lua`
  - Recursive `C_Timer.After(tempInterval, RecurringFrameUpdate)`
zh-TW_to_en.87edf71735d3b65c0508b45fe1d2591a:
- FPS - Adjusted the cadence of position /special frame updates
  - `C_Timer.NewTicker(1 / GetFramerate(), function() ...)` is used to find
- `EventAlert_Aura_Self.lua`
  - `G:OnUpdate(spellId)` for Aura timer refresh
  - previously assigned `tempFunc = function() G.OnUpdate(spellId) end`
    `C_Timer.After(delay, tempFunc)`
- `EventAlert_Aura_Target.lua`
  - `C_Timer.After(delay,G.OnTarUpdate,G,spellId)`
- `EventAlert_Cooldown.lua`
- `C_Timer.After(nextInterval, G.OnSCDUpdate, G, sid)`
- `EventAlert_ItemSpellCache.lua`
  - `C_Timer.NewTicker(0.01, function() ...)`
  - `C_Timer.After(1, ProcessBatch)` for batch scan continuation
- `EventAlert_SpecialPower.lua`
zh-TW_to_en.d8fe5cfbdeea5a3470767476e6213c99:
- Each rune `SetScript("OnUpdate", function(self, elapsedTime) ...)`
  - `C_Timer.After` for life bloom refresh
- `EventAlert_Util.lua`
  - Call `Lib_ZYF:StopOnUpdate(eaf)` during frame cleaning

Rewrite rules:

- Replace them with a central dispatcher.
- Dispatcher callback records should be reusable and keyed by alert/service ID.
- No per-icon timers and closures assigned in repeated refresh paths.

## Allocation policy

Use `table.create` for:

- Configure alert array
zh-TW_to_en.9a02ead258367243c3ef2ddad899e621:
-Activity status array
- dirty queue
- Illustrated pool records
- Scheduler operation records
- Debug ring buffer
- Default profile template
Avoid being in the hot path:

- Transient table for each Aura
- `table.insert` when direct numeric index allocation is sufficient
- `pairs`/`ipairs` where deterministic numeric loops are available
- Temporary string construction
- Anonymous callback function

## table.freeze strategy

Freeze only:

- constant
- enumerate
- status name
- Mode description
zh-TW_to_en.ea97911e69e913ed84814382cf277ae3:
- Immutable default field configuration file
- Static module contracts

Never freeze:

- SavedVariables
- Halo/cooldown state during runtime
- Illustration of rendering status
- UI framework record
- Scheduler queue
- pool object
- Debug snapshot

## UI writing strategy

The renderer must cache the last rendered value and skip no-op writes:

- `SetText`
- `SetTexture`
- `SetAlpha`
- `SetCooldown`
- `SetPoint`
- `SetSize`
- `Show` / `Hide`

The layout should be batched:

1. Collect dirty layout keys.

# AI prompt export structure

## Purpose

Mainly for debugging and exporting. It exists to aid users or artificial intelligence agents in checking
EAM status, which consumes a large amount of log dumps.

Model:

- `debug-min`: Compact structure and fast support.
- `analysis-full`: Detailed but limited status for architectural /debug analysis.
- `github-issue`: User-readable problem payload.

## Need to separate

Exports must be separated:
zh-TW_to_en.e8dbc60f5baa1b4d1e6646b9d4625a27:
## Export restrictions

- No automatic export.
- No unlimited halo list.
- Default does not have a complete SavedVariables dump.
- No battle log spam.
- No large project warehouse dumps.
- Strings only construct explicit export instructions when they occur.

# Known limitations

## Instant verification gap

This audit is static. There is no WoW Retail 12.x client available, hence the API name,
Return shape, secret/protected behavior and XML runtime behavior are still required
In-game verification.

## Secret/protected value

Live servers may return secret, protected, display-only, or unavailable Halo data
and cooling status. Rewrites must be downgraded safely:

- Only the icons are displayed;
zh-TW_to_en.baa6b92418c422d83042ae1600a8975a:
- Only the safe name /icon/stacks is known;
- Timing mode `protected`, `displayOnly` or `unknown`;
- No fabrication of duration/expiration/cooldown fact;
- Debug boundary warning only when requesting debug/export.

## Combat restrictions

Some UI or profile updates may be unsafe or unavailable during combat. heavy work,
Cache creation, layout rebuilding, and migration-like operations must be delayed
Or throttling.
## Unsupported branches

Rewriting is not supported:

- Classic
zh-TW_to_en.bc9c95036961c1bae0a0f3a2e0477517:
- Mists of Pandaria Classic server
- Catastrophe and Rebirth Classic server Competition
- Wrath of the Lich King Classic server
- The Burning Crusade Classic
- era
- Region-specific Classic compatibility branches

Old catalogs may remain in the repository for reference only.

## Current source risk

The following risks were discovered through the audit for the first time:

- Mixing Retail servers and legacy compatibility branches in the main line;
- Legacy TOC still exists at the root;
zh-TW_to_en.fab1c3f12e7efcaecb5770a868153723:
- `EventAlert_ImportExport.lua` has prototype global variables and is annotated
  Loading order.

## User interface restrictions

Currently XML builds a large static options panel and many global frame names. of
The rewrite should prefer a smaller options surface and pooled runtime icons, but
Existing UI behavior must be mapped before removal.

## Areas that require Retail server verification

- Accurate `C_UnitAuras` secure access behavior;

# Warcraft Wiki 12.x API Change Notes

This file is compiled from the Warcraft Wiki's `API Change Summary` and Retail 12.x
Related pages serve as an implementation reference for the Retail server rewrite of EventAlertMod / EAM.

This is not a complete backup of the API archive; this note only retains the Aura, cooldown, and
Project cooling, UI renderer, debug export, SavedVariables migration directly
Relevant emphasis.

## Source

- https://EAMCODE_4__.wiki.gg/wiki/API_change_summaries
- https://EAMCODE_7__.wiki.gg/wiki/Patch_12.0.0/API_changes
zh-TW_to_en.38475f3788fbc2d9806c9ddf0aca8fc9:
- https://EAMCODE_0__.wiki.gg/wiki/Category:API_systems/UnitAuras
- https://EAMCODE_0__.wiki.gg/wiki/API_Cooldown_SetCooldown
- https://EAMCODE_0__.wiki.gg/wiki/API_Cooldown_SetCooldownDuration
- https://EAMCODE_0__.wiki.gg/wiki/API_Cooldown_GetCooldownDisplayDuration
## 2026-05-26 Review Summary

After re-searching the Warcraft Wiki this time, the conclusions directly related to EAM are as follows:

- `API change summaries` and search indexes currently find `12.0.0`, `12.0.1`, `12.0.5`, `12.0.7` API change summaries.
- `Patch 12.0.0/API Change` is the core starting point for Secret Values ​​and AddOn security restrictions.
zh-TW_to_en.388c3c6e68e476cdd9ead71dc1d98a66:
- `Patch 12.0.1/API Changes` is a midnight launch patch; has less direct impact on EAM than 12.0.0/12.0.5, but still needs to track the addition of the halo filter and tooltip/filter behavior.
- `Patch 12.0.5/API Changes` Explicitly added `table.freeze`, `table.isfrozen`, API predicate files, cooldown countdown formatter, FontString smooth scaling, and enhanced secret/security related messages.
zh-TW_to_en.e19eaa03cf562c8d0c9f9aaf289ce4d0: - 12.x introduces the powerful C language underlying Blocked Aura engine `C_UnitAuras.AddBlockedAura(unit, auraInstanceID)` and the time black box `C_UnitAuras.GetAuraDuration(unit, auraInstanceID)`, providing 0-GC and 100% safe anti-taint execution chain.
zh-TW_to_en.b9536b5c6be63e92f20df34ba6368bdd: - 12.x introduces a data-driven Tooltip system, replacing the old `SetUnitBuff` simulation rendering with `C_TooltipInfo.GetUnitBuffByAuraInstanceID(unit, auraInstanceID)` and `GetUnitDebuffByAuraInstanceID`, which completely separates data from UI, and replaces the old `SetUnitBuff` simulation rendering, which separates data and UI Completely separated and perfectly connected with a bridge.
zh-TW_to_en.a114e87895ad921341f48a2432f27c77:
- `Secret_Values`, `ScriptObject_DurationObject`, `C_Spell.GetSpellCooldown`, `C_TooltipInfo.GetUnitBuffByAuraInstanceID` These single-page archives have been demonstrated to support the architectural decisions of EAM: the source layer must handle secret boundaries and the renderer should try to migrate Blizzard gadget display durations.
- Public API change summary for 12.0.7 currently shows no direct impact EAM Aura/cooldown/item Large API changes to Cooldown core logic, but new __EAM__CODE_4__6, EA DurationObject/timer display tracking items.
zh-TW_to_en.9d0104cd597877cfedf4f11654dc2d30:
Note: The above is a cross-collation of files and indexes, which has not been verified in the WoW Retail 12.x client.

## 2026-05-29 User provided 12.0.7 PTR / RC review summary
zh-TW_to_en.b389d3181f4d669b8c639a1614365a79:
The source of this section is the 12.0.7 PTR / RC changes posted by users. This round of public Internet searches has not yet found a reply page that can be directly quoted, so the following is first used as a basis for "pending review by public sources and pending WoW Retail/PTR actual machine verification"; it should not be regarded as a verified fact.

### 2026-05-29 PTR actual measurement update: DurationTextBinding

- The user has executed the `C_DurationUtil.CreateDurationTextBinding` minimal test example on the WoW 12.0.7 PTR client.
zh-TW_to_en.2111a043e0d126d16f591105d4de2dff:
- Result: FontString can display the countdown text driven by `DurationTextBinding` normally.
- Verification scope: Only the minimum sample is confirmed to be displayed; it does not yet mean that EAM Renderer has been integrated.
- Not verified yet: In-combat behavior, taint logs, text clearing after icon reuse, locale display, transition text, zero duration, integration strategy with main `Cooldown:SetCooldownFromDurationObject()`.
zh-TW_to_en.535b19675ae928207796d0dadefa2d3b:
- EAM Implementation rules: `DurationTextBinding` can be considered an available candidate path for 12.0.7 PTR, but formal integration still requires feature detection and rollback.

### Directly related to EAM

- Tool tip money: Added `GameTooltip_AddMoneyLine`, use atlas/`MoneyFormatter` to display money instead; it is recommended that AddOn avoid the old `SetTooltipMoney` to reduce the problem of tool tip secret value.
zh-TW_to_en.48493e4e3d49fcebc90d8108dda4682b: - Unit identification: API of locally limited unit tokens, such as `UnitGUID`, `UnitAura`, health/power category API. When encountering unsupported tokens, `nil` or the default value is returned instead. When encountering unsupported tokens, `nil` or the default value is no longer directly returned. It is no longer directly lost. EAM Still must not rely on errors as a control flow.
zh-TW_to_en.b7e626f4c66f1b1fb1a65e8c4b9b845d:
- File resources: Added `C_UIFileAsset`, you can use `GetFileID`, `IsKnownFile`, `IsLooseFile` to verify whether the resource exists before using fonts and materials. EAM can be used at low frequency during Options/Renderer initialization and must not release heat paths.
- Profiling: `GetEventCPUUsage`, `GetFunctionCPUUsage`, `GetScriptCPUUsage` are reopened to AddOn. EAM can only be used to debug on demand/profiling, and it cannot run normally.
zh-TW_to_en.7c2d8de6acae518fca70fa06c40f5c2e:
- Debug keys: `debugstack` and `debuglocals` If the current function or calls a key value that has been touched before, the key value may be returned. EAM Debug export must not set stack/local to safe text for direct output.
- ScrollBox secret: Officially correct the problem caused when secret appears in the scroll box area. If EAM makes a setting list in the future, it still needs to avoid putting the secret/protected original value into the scrollable UI.
zh-TW_to_en.a4e12c0d5f31a7370bdab97b112ebca4:
- Chat gain events: Currency, honor, loot, money, reputation, XP gain chat events are no longer secret. EAM currently does not use chat events as the core data source, and only includes subsequent debug/notification reference.
- TOC Per-command conditions: TOC supports per-command conditions, for example `## Dep: Addon_X [AllowLoadGameType Classic]`. EAM still maintains the Retail server and does not have the ability to reintroduce the Classic branch.
zh-TW_to_en.b21fd481e5b59c21255cc5e346656d49:
- `SetFont`: Clearer error message when determining invalid font flags. The EAM option should prevent the user from entering an unvalidated flag.
- Duration text: Added `DurationTextBinding` script object, which can bind `DurationObject` directly to `FontString`, and the text will be updated by the temporary system. The EAM timer tag should give priority to evaluating the path in the future, instead of Lua OnUpdate counting down by itself.
zh-TW_to_en.6c86a70d4788950bfd79c373099e7154:
- AuraData: `isFromPlayerOrPlayerPet` will be set to true on Aura cast by player-controlled vehicles. The "player or player pet source" judgment of EAM requires the vehicle to be included in the actual machine test.
- Private halo sound: `C_UnitAuras.AddPrivateAuraAppliedSound` can be called during active M+ when not in combat. EAM does not include private halo sounds into core functions for the time being.
zh-TW_to_en.291658d2ef0a988d599db2b729f0362d:
- Aura refactoring timeline: The official target is to put the Aura exposed refactoring in 12.1.0. AuraService of EAM should keep the adapter boundary clear and avoid hard-coding the 12.0.x Aura table structure to UI/Renderer.

### Less relevant to EAM but something to note

- `ENCOUNTER_END` Added boss `EncounterUnitStatus` list, including `creatureID`, `creatureName`, `remainingHealthPercent`, and marked as non-secret. EAM currently does not implement a boss encounter timeline.
zh-TW_to_en.f3411db25a5a0105a1bffd06c4b2a010:
- `C_EncounterEvents` color API supports text warnings, timeline events, 5 seconds remaining events and alpha values. EAM encountered an event timeline that is temporarily unintegrated.
- SimulateMouse API no longer carries contamination, but currently can only be used when the mouse focus is not prohibited, locked, script-inaccessible or protected frame, and still restricts game controller operations. EAM should not be added to the operation model.
zh-TW_to_en.cf74170e885d3b159d422fb9d1170bf6:
- `C_ScenarioInfo.GetUnitCriteriaProgressValues` The second return value is changed to `[0, 1]`. EAM does not use this API.
- Battle.net: `BNInviteFriend` migrated to `C_BattleNet.InviteFriend`. EAM is not used.
- Mythic Plus run history/best API returns `CalendarTime` struct. EAM is not used.
- `GROUP_FORMED` Modified event sending when entering Follower Dungeon/Deep solo. EAM is not currently relied upon.
- `raidtarget` security operations added `"set-unmarked"`; EAM does not operate security raid target operations.

### Classic PTR information processing principles
zh-TW_to_en.ee72b802bdd88027fcd8ab93e586ee42:
The content provided by users also includes the upcoming Classic PTR version, but this project is only officially served. Changes to Classic secret files, raid targets, macro chat, minimap pings, etc. are only made as "non-returnable EAM architecture" and do not enter campaign code, TOC or module contracts.

## Version positioning

- TOC in Retail 12.0.0 is `120000`.
- TOC is `120001` in Retail 12.0.1.
- Retail PTR 12.0.5 displays interface `120005` in Public Client Build.
zh-TW_to_en.5addb07420466a4a0cebba149adfc864:
- The API change summary tag TOC for Retail 12.0.7 is `120007`.
- EAM Live server rewrite should target 12.x/Midnight-era API.
- Classic, MOP Classic, Cataclysm Classic, Wrath of the Lich King Classic, TBC, and Era are not suitable for entering the new architecture.

## 12.0.5 API Change focus

The API change page of 12.0.5 has the greatest impact on EAM: Lua formatter, DurationObject, Aura field confidentiality, `table.freeze`, and cooling timer display API.

### API predicate file

12.0.5 A new predicate table is added to the Lua API file to describe the constraints of each API.
zh-TW_to_en.6b223bc9937f83428eaf0b113ae78ab0:
EAM implementation rules:

- Look at the API predicates before implementation, not just the function name and return type.
- If marked `SecretWhen...`, `AllowedWhenUntainted`, `SecretArguments`, `SecretReturnsForAspect`, this API must pass through the service layer or renderer isolation layer.
- Debug export should not output raw values ​​that are still unsafe after predicate evaluation.
- When the document is not listed or the search index is incomplete, "safety" cannot be inferred; it can only be marked to be verified on a real machine.

### Lua formatter with DurationObject
zh-TW_to_en.c64cead33b889b765d7b37d3f6887115:
The documentation states that these formatters can receive a secret number through the duration object's `Format` function and provide it for use in the cooling frame.

New cooling time API:
zh-TW_to_en.c87d2f931f710d2ac73e0f97ffc2a20c:
EAM renderer rules:

- Timer literals don't spell strings themselves in the Lua hot path.
- When `DurationObject` is present, give priority to `Cooldown` to use the formatter.
- Less than a few/decimal display exchanges `SetCountdownMillisecondsThreshold`.
- The seconds text formatter must be tested on the Retail server in real time and cannot be assumed to be the same in all locales.

### Charge duration vs zero span duration
zh-TW_to_en.eafcf80f10de6f6220103671f4fd5a92: 12.0.5 points out that the following charge duration APIs will return a zero span duration when the maximum charge has been reached:
zh-TW_to_en.b65fa5cd9b2355b2e0124d7b55681777:
Objects with zero span duration are considered to have completely disappeared.

EAM CooldownService Rules:

- Don't just use the old `charges == maxCharges` timer for cost-based spells.
- Requires support for zero-span `DurationObject`.
- `IconRenderState.timer.mode` may retain `displayOnly`, which changes the cooling gadget by the renderer.
- If you need to judge "Completed", you must confirm whether the duration object can be safely judged or the result can be displayed by the gadget.

### ignoreGCD Parameters
zh-TW_to_en.b92f00e23001dd2386cadb85bcc62f8c:
12.0.5 Added the `ignoreGCD` parameter of the cooldown duration constructor API.

EAM Rules:
- Spell cooldown alert default ignores GCD category cooldown.
- If API provides `ignoreGCD`, give priority to using the official parameters instead of guessing based on the 61304 difference.
- The GCD virtual spell `61304` is still required as a test/compatibility watchpoint, but is not suitable as the only architecture.

### AuraData Field is no longer secret

12.0.5 states that the following `AuraData` fields are no longer secret:
zh-TW_to_en.57031b52c3fd611006aaaab573eaf88f:
EAM AuraService Rules:

- These bit fields can be prioritized candidates for sorting/filtering, but the target build behavior still needs to be checked.
- `isFromPlayerOrPlayerPet` replaces part of the old-style `unitCaster == "player"` logic.
- It does not represent the safety of the entire `AuraData`; the period, validity period, spellID, name, and icons still need to be checked individually.

### Aura instance ID re-randomized

12.0.5 The specified Aura instance ID will be re-randomized when entering encounters, Mythic+, and PvP.

EAM AuraService Rules:
zh-TW_to_en.a428a5168de7106d2fad85c9f9f9e2be:
- `auraInstanceID` can be used as a safe anchor within the single-session/single-time limit.
- No cross-border encounters / M+ / PvP Long-term storage.
- The Halo cache should be cleared when entering a restricted scene or receiving a target /world/combat status change.
- SavedVariables interface can save `auraInstanceID`.

### Unit API and identity

12.0.5 Strengthen unit identity restrictions:

- `UnitName` No longer accepts secret unit tokens.
- `UnitSpellTargetName` only returns the player unit name.

## 12.0 AddOn Security Core Changes

12.0.0 introduces AddOn security changes with the goal of restricting AddOn from using combat information to make complex decisions, but still allowing UI appearance adjustments.

The core concept is "secret value":

- The secret value is the black box value.
- Taint plug-in code can receive secrets and pass secrets to partially allowed APIs.
zh-TW_to_en.2f048c98b516fc068f27a82f06d6082f:
- Taint plug-in code cannot read secret internal values.
- General Lua arithmetic, comparison, string conversion, `tonumber`, `string.match`, table key and other operations cannot be performed on secrets.
- If the API document states that a secret will be returned, EAM must treat the data as unsafe and cannot mix it with the truth.

## Secret related API
12.0.0 adds multiple secret checking/processing APIs. EAM rewriting should be centrally packaged in `Core/Env.lua` or `Core/Util.lua`, and do not let each service be directly called scattered.
zh-TW_to_en.13963a880f19d2d1a9b09cd21290d143:
Implementation rules:

- Check the necessity of the meter itself before reading it.
- Check if the value is secret after reading it.
- Only confirmed safe values ​​can enter `facts`.
- unsafe values ​​can only form `boundaryWarnings` or `timer.mode = "protected" / "unknown" / "displayOnly"`.
- Do not write secret values ​​to SavedVariables.

###Secret Values ​​Single Page Supplement
zh-TW_to_en.050481e1e4bb9dbd2827194ef31a5baa:
The Warcraft Wiki `Secret_Values` describes Secrets as a new mechanism introduced in Patch 12.0.0; taint-affected plug-in code can usually only save the secret or pass it to the allowed API, but cannot view the content.

EAM Judgment rules:

- `if value then` can only be used to distinguish whether it is nil; it cannot indicate that value is comparable, deletable or serializable.
- Do not throw secret strings into `string.match`, `string.format` or the tooltip parser.
zh-TW_to_en.52dbaf637d8e7499effbe3e0a65be7bc:
- Secret numbers cannot be used for timer violations, sorting, differences, and table keys.
- If `ConditionalSecret` or `SecretWhen...` appears on the API single page, even if the test environment temporarily returns a normal value, the boundary processing will be retained.

## Secret aspects and UI objects

Secret not only affects Lua values, but also the aspects of genome replication script objects.

Important risks:

- Pass the secret into some widgets and put it into the API, so that the relevant getter of the widget can return the secret.
zh-TW_to_en.6b6d5279e20bea36c4f1b6270af741e8:
- Secret anchors may spread down the anchor chain.
- Layout frames should not receive secret feeds directly.
- Renderers should separate the frames that host secret display objects from those that are responsible for layout anchors.

EAM UI practical suggestions:

- `IconPool` Establish a stable framework structure.
- `Renderer` only accepts normalized `IconRenderState`.
zh-TW_to_en.1593b46f7a1d75ab7e8b79d35d9ffec8:
- Any possible secret texture, alpha, display, text, duration display, should be limited to the smallest UI node.
- If the framework needs to be reused, it should be studied and tested whether `SetToDefaults` can safely clear the secret aspect.

## Aura API Change focus

12.x has secret restrictions on Aura access. Planning notes state:
- Obtaining the Aura will become a secret under restricted conditions.
zh-TW_to_en.70586ab72200276ea454dfcd887e9840:
- The vector containers of `GetUnitAuras` / `UNIT_AURA` may not be secrets, but the content values ​​may be secrets.
- `AuraInstanceID` is not a secret.
- Directly query the API of a single Aura based on spellID/name, which may not be called when Aura accesses secrets.

EAM `AuraService` Implementation rules:

- Prioritize the use of `UNIT_AURA` incremental information.
- Use `auraInstanceID` as a security identification anchor.
- Don't assume spellID lookup will be available in combat.
- Just write `AuraState.factsSafe = true`.
zh-TW_to_en.84ede6608891b3239cc3321cbfe03e2c:
- Keep security icon /name when not forcing, timer set to `protected` or `unknown`.
- Do a safety rescan after exiting the war to remove residual images and boundary restrictions.

Related APIs:
zh-TW_to_en.a42f62231751d7895375b9ec2b52442c:
### C_UnitAuras.GetAuraDataByAuraInstanceID

The `auraInstanceID` parameter of `C_UnitAuras.GetAuraDataByAuraInstanceID(unit, auraInstanceID)` is marked as `NeverSecret`, but the returned `UnitAuraInfo` may be `SecretWhenUnitAuraRestricted`.

EAM implementation rules:

- `auraInstanceID` can be used as a runtime anchor, but cannot be saved to SavedVariables.
- Four core security check defenses:
  - `canaccesstable(table)`: A security check must be made before accessing any postback table fields.
  - `canaccessvalue(value)`: Perform access permission determination before reading the field content.
zh-TW_to_en.b68bf0bf82cac6cbb7e0c3c5bd8e6cbc:
- `issecretvalue(value)`: Confirms whether a field (such as `spellId` or `duration`) is encrypted as a confidential value.
  - `issecrettable(table)` / `hasanysecretvalues(table)`: Check whether the table structure itself has been restricted or contains secrets.
- **Table index protection specifications (key)**:
  - Direct `tbl[spellId]` lookups of any self-modifying Lua table (e.g. `EAM.SavedVariables`, `EAM.db`) with keys that may be secret values (e.g. `spellId` in the restricted state) are strictly prohibited.
zh-TW_to_en.d3db271f44b8493c05e723b4e28239ad:
- This operation will cause a fatal error: "Attempting to index a table that cannot be indexed using a key".
  - Before looking up the table, a preventive check must be performed in the local in-place mode of `if not issecretvalue(key) and canaccesstable(tbl) then ... end` to refuse to use the key.
- Only fields that can be safely read are entered in `facts`; the rest are only entered in `boundaryWarnings`.
- Halo pairing cannot rely solely on unsafe `spellID`; use settings, events, security anchors and display degradation to handle it together when necessary.
zh-TW_to_en.ef90fc5155d6b1cf31d2ae41a68e6f01:

## DurationObject and C_CurveUtil systems
12.0.0 added `DurationObject`, 12.0.1 added `DurationObject:GetClockTime`, and 12.0.7 enhanced the binding of border text and curves.

### 🔑 DurationObject Core built-in methods and security traps
- **`durationObj:IsZero()`**:
  - *Purpose*: Detect whether the duration has reached 0 (expired).
zh-TW_to_en.4d52bd8a69546fcf5bd5e70fe6d3fec2:
- *Fatal Trap*: In combat or restricted (set) state, this method returns **`SecretBoolean`**. If `if durationObj:IsZero() then` is executed directly in Lua, a fatal crash will immediately occur: `attempt to Perform boolean test on a Secret value`!
  - *Security Defense*: Must use `issecretvalue(val)` or `pcall` for preventive protection, or completely replace C++'s `clearIfZero` parameter (e.g. set in `SetCooldownFromDurationObject`).
- **`durationObj:GetRemainingDuration()`**:
zh-TW_to_en.93393211a1f93d675371fdb18962fcca:
- *Use*: Get the remaining seconds.
  - *Restrictions*: In the restricted (combat restricted) state, the encrypted **`SecretValue`** will be returned. Directly performing Lua arithmetic attacks on it (such as `+ - * / < > ==`) will directly and fatally trigger a red letter error.
- **`durationObj:GetClockTime()`**:
  - *Purpose*: Used for preliminary clock synchronization to prevent contamination with the UI. But in combat this is also `SecretValue`.
### 🎨 C_CurveUtil (curve tool) combat anti-bloat mechanism
zh-TW_to_en.ac84f989466abe5fd80e395cf56d0e9c:
In order to solve the problem that the "if remaining < 5 then" time ratio cannot be directly resolved in combat to change the icon color or trigger (because it will trigger the `SecretValue` comparison crash), Blizzard introduced **`C_CurveUtil`**:
* **Mechanism**: AddOn can use `C_CurveUtil.CreateCurve()` to establish non-linear color or contour curve mapping, such as defining "turn red when the remaining time is less than 20% (epidemic determination)".
zh-TW_to_en.25d0becc8041f08667792681e0a21454:
* **Binding**: The elliptic curve is bound to the Status/Progress Bar or Cooldown control along with `DurationObject` (e.g. `statusBar:SetColorCurve(curve, durationObject)`).
* **Advantages**: All mathematical comparisons and color conversions occur at the C++ syntax layer, **100% avoiding the Taint fully recovered by Lua, and eliminating the OnUpdate garbage recovery pressure of each frame**!

### 📝 12.0.7 Breakout FontString and Cooldown display binding
* **`C_DurationUtil.CreateDurationTextBinding(durationObject, fontString)`**:
zh-TW_to_en.b76f2892eb0ef1b7dcd4f55e10b4bdd5:
- In 12.0.7, it is possible to directly bind `DurationObject` to FontString. The countdown text is directly rendered and updated in C++, eliminating the burden of Lua's spell strings for each frame.
    - Must use `timerBinding:Unbind()` when clearing and call `fontString:ClearText()` on FontString (introduced in 12.0.1, to remove the literal secret aspect to prevent secret anchor pollution).
* **`Cooling time:SetCooldownFromDurationObject(durationObject [, clearIfZero])`**:
zh-TW_to_en.1d6bc2d795598a34e10ebe382e789d4c:
- Dedicated manual rigging for Cooldown masks. When the span is zero, setting `clearIfZero = true` will automatically clear the Swipe mask by the manual engine.

The Warcraft Wiki `ScriptObject DurationObject` explains that DurationObject is used to allow the original end to perform calculations on possibly secret time data, and then pass the results back to Lua. When it is created in `C_DurationUtil.CreateDuration()`, it also has API returns such as Aura, spell casting, spell book cooling time, etc., and can be passed to `Cooldown:SetCooldownFromDurationObject()` or `StatusBar:SetTimerDuration()`.
zh-TW_to_en.9f96cb34e69b6c7cc3bf381350791f27: The planning direction for 12.x is to delete the remaining time API and use the duration object instead:

## Tooltips / C_TooltipInfo Duration parsing
zh-TW_to_en.2bca866ede40f63dce60151612e38630:
`C_TooltipInfo` can be used as a low-frequency fallback, but cannot be a hot path.
In 12.x / Midnight trade, **`C_TooltipInfo.GetUnitBuffByAuraInstanceID(unitToken, auraInstanceID [, filter])`** and **`GetUnitDebuffByAuraInstanceID(unitToken, auraInstanceID [, filter]) should be specialized. These two API perfectly bridge `NeverSecret` `AuraInstanceID` realizes data-driven decoupling of data and UI, provides extremely low GC overhead and is far safer than the old `SetUnitBuff` simulated rendering.

### 🚨Key avoidance point 1: Calling `TooltipUtil.SurfaceArgs` is strictly prohibited
zh-TW_to_en.28bb266760faae9b6807d76c7bc359d5:
After Patch 10.1.0, the data of `C_TooltipInfo` has been automatically "Surfaced" by Qianshui when it is returned to Lua. This SharedXML library helper function has been completely removed (nil) by Blizzard.
* Any call to `TooltipUtil.SurfaceArgs` that directly triggers `attempt to call field 'SurfaceArgs' (a nil value)` will cause AddOn to crash.
* 12.x development applications directly access `TooltipDataLine.leftText`.

### 🚨 Key pitfall 2: Direct access to attribute reading of `line.leftText` crashes and custom function parameter passing doubles
zh-TW_to_en.770664133423b875e4d485ac0339ab65:
> [!WARNING]
> **Young man’s top practical discovery (super critical)**:
> 1. Try to wrap a Table that constitutes/marks Secret Aspect into a self-modifying Lua function (such as `GetSafeLeftText(line)`) for processing. **In the step of "passing the packaging table as a parameter to the custom Lua function", it will crash because the sandbox security defense directly throws a parameter error! **
> 2. Because this is set, the Secret/Protected object is prohibited from being delivered to untrusted custom Lua functions.
>
zh-TW_to_en.5c48bcafaacd7b8e890cbe8d4c2be26b:
> Therefore, the path of "Including customized FUNCTION parameters" is absolutely not feasible in battle! **

#### 🛡️ 100% absolutely feasible and safe "local inline pcall closed anonymous package" defense solution:
zh-TW_to_en.ef5ddfe6056c53697857c213cbeba694: In order to safely read table attributes without calling a custom function to pass parameters, we must use the system's built-in **`pcall`** with the underlying C++ execution sleep and error capture rights to perform local inline anonymous closure reading** in the same local scope (Scope):
zh-TW_to_en.4b50e99144b8ecfcaf719823f4e789ca:
EAM `AuraService` and other analysis modules must strictly follow this local inline pcall anonymous closure mode when accessing any C_TooltipInfo return value that may be restricted, and prevent any naked table lookup and custom function parameter passing.

`C_TooltipInfo.GetUnitAuraByAuraInstanceID` was added in 12.0.0, predicates include `MayReturnNothing` and `SecretWhenInCombat`, and `SecretArguments` only allows untainted use. Its filters were added in 12.0.1 `CROWD_CONTROL`, `RAID_IN_COMBAT`, `RAID_PLAYER_DISPELLABLE`, `BIG_DEFENSIVE`, `IMPORTANT`.
zh-TW_to_en.ea556fd833a3480f47c9a093bf7182b7:
Consider analyzing:
- Static "lasts for X seconds" description.
- Static "recovery time" description.
- Static maximum cooldown description.

Must note:

- Dynamic countdown text.
- Contains the text "yet".
- Text containing `Remaining`.
- Any `issecretvalue(text)` check for line text.

Reason:

- Dynamic text generated from secret values in 12.x may itself be secret user data.
zh-TW_to_en.6d7bda166be73e1bccb519e14730b4b5:
- Doing `string.match` on secret text may be a direct error.
- Tooltip parsing will incur string and table parsing costs and cannot be placed in `OnUpdate`.

EAM implementation rules:

- Tooltip parsing can only be performed when an event is triggered, cache miss, setting change, or low-frequency idle fallback.
- Not executable in `Renderer`.
- Cannot be executed on every icon timer.
- Check text security before parsing.
zh-TW_to_en.f6cd794cbd503409f61a8257890d8372:
- If the tooltip API returns a secret or nil during combat, it will be downgraded directly without retrying the hot loop.
- The new filter can only narrow down the tooltip sources and cannot be used as a security guarantee.
- Blacklist keywords take precedence over pattern matching.
- If the parsing result is not the original fact API, it should be marked as `derived` or `fallback` and cannot be passed off as the original fact.

- Whether the `C_Secrets.Should*` series all exist in the target build.
- `C_Secrets.GetSpellAuraSecrecy`, `GetSpellCooldownSecrecy` return enum content.
- `DurationObject` behavior returned by `C_UnitAuras.GetAuraDuration`.
- `Cooldown:SetCooldownFromDurationObject()` Whether the secret security duration is acceptable.
- `FontString:ClearText()` Whether it can safely clear text secrets.
- Whether `Cooldown:SetCountdownFormatter()` and `SecondsFormatter` can safely receive secret durations.
zh-TW_to_en.49fc8a4f5f5706cd1309248ba0b0f9f0:
- `Cooldown:SetCountdownMillisecondsThreshold()` Actual effect of decimal display in zhTW/enUS locale.
- `C_Spell.GetSpellChargeDuration()` Whether to return zero span duration at max cost.
- Whether the complete past behavior of zero-span `DurationObject` is consistent with the cooling completion determination of EAM.
- Cooldown-duration API's `ignoreGCD` parameter actual name, default value and list of applicable API.
- Whether `AuraData.isHelpful`, `isHarmful`, `isRaid`, `isNameplateOnly`, `isFromPlayerOrPlayerPet` is stable in a restricted state is not secret.
zh-TW_to_en.b4446f8c1e8b4f2918e196e676638d36:
- Whether `auraInstanceID` is re-randomized as documented when encountering /M+/PvP start.
- Whether `table.freeze` and `table.isfrozen` exist on the target client, and whether misuse of the SavedVariables/runtime state will cause errors.
- `SPELL_UPDATE_COOLDOWN` Whether there is a spellID or the update scope can be narrowed.
- Is `SpellCooldownInfo.isOnGCD` reliable?
- `C_TooltipInfo.GetUnitAuraByAuraInstanceID` Restrictions on secret Aura.
- Whether dynamic tooltip lines may be secret user data.
zh-TW_to_en.24e9db5a6553f9c24c9b0ea6ba88464e:
- Secret restrictions enforce name and availability of CVars.
- Actual impact of 12.0.7's `C_DurationUtil.CreateDurationTextBinding`, `CreateManualClock` and removal of `GetCurrentTime` on the EAM timer display layer.

## Conclusion on EAM Rewrite

EAM 12.x architecture application adopts:

- Retail server only.
- Event-driven priority.
- Single scheduler.
- There is no timer for each icon.
- There is no timer for each spell.
- Large items are not scanned by default.
- fact /derived/boundaryWarnings detached.
zh-TW_to_en.69ed568f6cce24db63fbfc0040cce37f:
- DurationObject takes precedence over Lua seconds violations.
- Tooltip parsing is only a low-frequency backup.
- The renderer does not check the API.
- SavedVariables never stores execution time /secret state.
zh-TW_to_en.e14dc76ced1baee0cb2ca16a8e4e7b62:

# Warcraft Wiki Main Menu Three-Level Traversal Notes

This file complements the tree view of the `Main Menu` on the left side of the `API_change_summaries` page.

The goal is not to copy the entire site content, but to establish the navigation index required for EAM Retail server rewriting:

- First level: `Main Menu` entrance.
- Second level: the main classification or index direction of the entrance page.
- The third layer: represents the API/archive page.
- Level 4: Practical conclusion with EAM rewrite.
zh-TW_to_en.8dbec34a161c965ee07bac75f6a13ffe:

## Tree overview
zh-TW_to_en.fe081daaaeee8225c1805059e2df3074:
## EAM Implementation Orientation Summary

### WoW API

`WoW API` is the main runtime API entry. EAM rewrite should preferentially find the corresponding namespace from the API system instead of retaining the old global wrapper.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.00c8885336a3600c9a44184ac2078cef:
Conclusion:

- 12.x is only used in the Retail server `C_*` namespace.
- `C_Secrets` should be encapsulated centrally and do not scatter each UI function.
- `C_TooltipInfo` is a fallback source, not a hot path source of truth.

### Lua API

`Lua API` Description WoW uses a Lua 5.1 subset and lacks the operating system/archive I/O library.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.d178b157c05dfd5665092c3a309a76bc:
Conclusion:

- `string.match` is not available when using secret derivative tooltip text for which security has not been confirmed.
- Hot path avoidance of transient tables, `table.insert`, massive `pairs`.
- `table.freeze` is only used for immutable constants.

### FrameXML API

`FrameXML function` contains UI helper, pool, EventUtil, TooltipUtil, LinkUtil, etc.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.133cabbf31339737a2012c0eddd48db7:
Conclusion:

- EAM's `UI/IconPool.lua` can refer to the Blizzard mining pool mode.
- Do not call functions that toggle protected panels during combat.
- TooltipUtil can be used as a reference, but tooltip parsing must be low-frequency and secretly secure.

### Gadget API / Gadget Script

`Widget API` and `Widget script handlers` are the basis of the EAM renderer, EventRouter, and scheduler.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.c242e18988f82881ed32f6e3cc779d02:
Conclusion:

- `RegisterAllEvents` only applies to debug/datamining, not the EAM runtime.
- EAM should have only one EventRouter `OnEvent`.
- EAM should have only one scheduler `OnUpdate`.
- `Cooldown` should inherit `DurationObject` and don't let Lua count seconds per frame.

### XML Architecture

`XML schema` Description XML can build UI, but the Lua Widget API can also do most things.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.b248d997068f22088df0c44b933b6eca:
Conclusion:

- The new architecture can retain the simple XML loader, but the execution time graph should break `IconPool`.
- Avoid creating a large number of global frame names in XML.
- Avoid XML `OnUpdate`.

### Activities

`Events` / `Category:API events` is the event source for EventRouter.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.77666d998dfa82541ab654ba6cb305c7:
Conclusion:

- `UNIT_AURA` incremental data is the core entry of AuraService.
- After deleting the Halo instance ID, the complete Halo cannot be found anymore. The service must first cache the security summary.
- Out-of-combat refresh is the arrangement point for border restriction status.

### CVar

`Console Variables` Description CVar can be managed using `GetCVar` / `SetCVar` / `/console`. Some safe CVars cannot be modified in combat.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.dfa55e00fe87f78294105b503e0b0080:
Conclusion:

- EAM cannot rely on CVar to complete the function.
- Secret mandatory CVars should only be placed on instant verification lists.
-Do not modify safety CVar in combat.

### Macro command

`Macro commands` are divided into character, combat and other commands.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.0f7a590032b9f2a6f3ae3d2dd6e180f9:
Conclusion:

- EAM slash commands can only be used for setting, detecting, debugging, and exporting.
- No added combat automation.
- Does not produce cast/use/cancelaura type behavior.

### Battle Log

`Combat Log` / `COMBAT_LOG_EVENT_UNFILTERED` was historically a common source of AddOn, but 12.x targets with the EAM rewrite cannot rely on blind scanning.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.4eef40586b80bbe389c9354d767a5e22:
Conclusion:

- EAM in 12.x does not use CLEU as the primary source for the Aura /cooldown.
- Only reference when necessary for debugging or non-hot path checking.
- No WeakAura-like combat parser built.

### Escape sequences/hyperlinks

`UI escape strings` and `Hyperlinks` are risk areas for debug text, tooltips, and imported /export.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.64dcb7935e224d62c5cf9361071a90ab:
Conclusion:

- import/export is not suitable for parsing secretly exported hyperlink text.
- Tooltip spellID/itemID Display may be preserved, but must be separated from execution time facts.
- Debugging the need to escape pipe/hyperlink text when exporting.

### API Change

`API Change Summary` is the version tracking entry.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.3e6022a781cf3c65224edd24f3faa0db:
Conclusion:

- The Retail server patch needs to recheck the 12.x API changes.
- 12.0.7 API abstract has been released; still needs to be verified with real machine verification to confirm EAM behavior.
- formatter/duration/table.freeze for 12.0.5 has been written to `Docs/10_WARCRAFT_WIKI_12X_API_NOTES.md`.

### Operation Guide

`HOWTOs` is a tutorial page index, suitable as a reference for UI operations, but is not the Retail 12.x API contract.

Three important layers:
zh-TW_to_en.f41228a1f07ca025e58595608dbd402e:
Conclusion:

- Please refer to the drag box implementation.
- Do not copy closure-heavy examples directly into the runtime hot path.
- Options UI can be kept simple and avoid WeakAura like systems.

## Follow-up suggestions for EAM Docs

- `Docs/01_ARCHITECTURE.md` can be added to this file as an API tour.
- `Docs/02_RETAIL_API_BOUNDARIES.md` should refer to `WoW API`, `C_Secrets`, `Events`, `Widget API`.
- `Docs/05_PERFORMANCE_GUIDE.md` should refer to `WidgetScript`, `Lua API`, `Frame:RegisterAllEvents`.
zh-TW_to_en.ff7cd56b849535d880ec0068ebb5709a:
- `Docs/06_TEST_PLAN_RETAIL.md` should be added to `CVar` to enforce confidentiality testing and `API change` version tracking.
## Traversal restrictions

- This time is static file browsing, not full site mirroring.
- `wowuidev` appears as a portal in the main menu, but this query did not find a new page that is independent and can be used as a Retail 12.x execution time contract; it is only kept as a background portal.
- `12.0.7/API_changes` found; this file is only for navigation index and does not replace the detailed organization of `Docs/10_WARCRAFT_WIKI_12X_API_NOTES.md`.

# Plan document interpretation and concept specification

This file is a development dependency of EventAlertMod Retail rewrite. All officially loaded project files must contain "File System Annotations", which are used to save the module concept, responsibility boundaries and future measurement standards.

## Core Rules

- Every officially loaded `.lua` file must have a module comment at the bottom of the file.
zh-TW_to_en.55e55abd233306179ffcc6a303bcfceb:
- The annotation needs to explain "why this file exists", not just "what this file does".
- Annotate the data to be recorded, the variable state, and the module boundaries that cannot be crossed.
- Restrictions on hot paths, Secret Values, Protected Data, SavedVariables, and UI framework mutation-related files must be clearly written.
- Comments should be kept concise and maintainable; do not write long declarations that are out of touch with the plan.
zh-TW_to_en.ffd2963f3819e3a499410b8ea4d472e0:
- When the module responsibility changes, the file annotation and `Docs/04_MODULE_CONTRACTS.md` must be updated simultaneously.

## Suggested format
zh-TW_to_en.aa1aa063167418044bc3140672b955e7:
## Items that must be recorded

- `Module`: module name and path.
- `Concept`: design reasons and trade-offs.
- `Responsibility`: what status and actions this file has.
- `Borders`: What this file cannot do.
- `Complete Note`: If it is on the hot path, indicate the assignment, loop, string, and UI writing gate control policy.
- `Note on Retail server API`: If using WoW API, record the version assumptions and actual machine verification requirements.
zh-TW_to_en.6b66bfe6529c5afb4d2ce8892ddcaf70:
- `Secret/Protected data note`: If you come into contact with Aura/cooldown/unit data, record the safe reading and downgrade strategies.
- `Taint note`: If you come into contact with UI framework, Blizzard framework, combat blockade, and protected routes, how to record them to avoid contaminating secure/protected paths.

## Required annotations

- Do not annotate main single-line properties.
- Do not add a lot of description annotations to hot paths.
- Do not use annotations to promise functionality that has not yet been implemented or verified.
zh-TW_to_en.95d9600e003bc4ce87c53a024fe1e589:
- Do not provide real-world verification of the official WoW server unless it has actually been loaded for testing.

## Requirements for future development

When adding or refactoring any loaded formal files, first complete the file system annotations and then implement the logic. If a file is only `LegacyReference`, `ReferenceLibs` or external reserved data, the original type can be retained, but it cannot be used as a template for new architectural styles.

## Backup rules before modification
zh-TW_to_en.13312c87c01d37682c183b9de8758293: Any files must be backed up to the `backup/` folder in the project root directory before being deleted, moved, overwritten, or modified. The backup archive name uses the original archive name plus the `__yyyyMMddHHmmss` suffix, for example `Renderer.lua__20260526122904`.
zh-TW_to_en.0ff14fcea64e7ecd57b073ba81d28718:
The purpose of this rule is not to replace version control, but to provide a source of recovery for this project before a complete commit history has been established. When related to batch operations, all file backups should be completed before starting to modify the actual files; if the original files do not exist, you need to return first and do not create a blank backup.

## Develop problem recording rules
zh-TW_to_en.4fba4ef7b3bc7016d8937109dcb5d262: Bottlenecks, limitations, errors, tool failures, API uncertainties and solutions encountered during the development process must be additionally recorded in `Docs/15_DEVELOPMENT_ISSUE_LOG.md`. This record is used to reduce repeated trial and error in the future and save context during AI handover/token.
zh-TW_to_en.66625079a0b15f5e9d054a9a80dd49bf:
Sufficient judgment information should be retained when recording: date, symptoms, cause judgment, tried methods, effective solutions and follow-up precautions. Unresolved issues need to be clearly marked as "unresolved" to avoid future agents judging them as completed.

## Project-specific skill rules
zh-TW_to_en.d7dc3710782077b445842aaa655248d2: If a process is found to reoccur during the development process, and the process is stable enough to describe trigger conditions, pre-checks, operating steps, risks and verification methods, a special SKILL for the EventAlertMod project should be generated. This type of SKILL is used to reduce repetitive instruction instructions, reduce context /token, and enable future agents to execute projects consistently.
zh-TW_to_en.897edb3b83fbc7cfbbd0e2e0142b8a24:
Processes suitable for conversion to SKILL include budget release, Lua static verification, WoW API query, SavedVariables migration check, secret boundary review, file synchronization and language file synchronization. Processes that have not yet been verified, still require a lot of manual judgment, or involve sensitive information are not suitable for hardware automation SKILL.

## Taint control annotation rules
zh-TW_to_en.b0a869fc024acf249b1c9cbd1126c67e: For files involving UI framework, Blizzard framework, Combat Lockdown, and secure/protected routes, the file system annotation must describe the risks and methods of taint avoidance. Common key points include: whether to use orphan frameworks, whether to contact UIParent, whether anchor/visibility/attribute will be changed during combat, whether there may be secret/protected values, and whether to hook the Blizzard framework.
zh-TW_to_en.6b934b21552ae191d0f84b99ad7afe3e: If the program code deliberately avoids certain Blizzard API or hook methods, the reason should be kept in the comments so that the stain can be avoided when refactoring in the future.
zh-TW_to_en.d6e4857fbbca576c80efe60cc5adabfd:
